name: Unemployment Insurance (UI)
source: MN Statutes Chapter 268; MN Department of Employment and Economic Development (DEED)
description:
Minnesota Unemployment Insurance (UI) provides temporary, partial wage replacement to workers who become unemployed through no fault of their own. The program is funded entirely by taxes on employers — workers do not pay into UI. Benefits help unemployed workers meet basic needs while they actively search for new employment.

To qualify, a person must have lost their job through no fault of their own. This typically means being laid off due to lack of work, position elimination, or business closure. People who quit voluntarily generally do not qualify unless they had good cause attributable to the employer (such as unsafe working conditions, significant wage reduction, or harassment). People fired for misconduct may be disqualified.

Applicants must have sufficient work history. Minnesota uses a base period — the first four of the last five completed calendar quarters before filing. Applicants must have earned wages in at least two quarters of the base period, and total base period wages must be at least 5.3% of the statewide average annual wage (approximately $4,500 in recent years, subject to annual update). Applicants must also have earned at least $2,400 in their highest-earning quarter.

Weekly benefit amounts are calculated at approximately 50% of the applicant's average weekly wage during the base period, up to a maximum of $857 per week (2024 figure, adjusted annually). The minimum weekly benefit is $30. Most claimants receive benefits for up to 26 weeks in a benefit year, though extended benefits may be available during periods of high unemployment.

To continue receiving benefits, claimants must certify weekly that they are able to work, available for work, and actively seeking employment (typically at least three documented employer contacts per week). Claimants must report any earnings from part-time or temporary work, which may reduce but not eliminate benefits.

Applications should be filed online at uimn.org as soon as possible after job loss. There is a one-week waiting period before benefits begin. Filing is done online or by phone; weekly certifications must be completed each week to continue receiving payments.
eligibility_summary: Lost job through no fault of own (layoff, position elimination). Sufficient work history: earned wages in at least 2 of 4 base period quarters, at least $4,500 total base period wages (approximate), at least $2,400 in highest quarter. Available and actively seeking work (3 employer contacts/week). Minnesota resident or worked in MN. No income limit — based on wage history.
benefit_amounts: Weekly benefit: approximately 50% of average weekly wage during base period. Maximum $857/week (2024, adjusted annually). Minimum $30/week. Up to 26 weeks standard duration.
documents_required: Social Security number, contact information for all employers in the past 18 months, reason for separation from each employer, alien registration number (if applicable), direct deposit information.
contact: DEED UI Customer Service: 651-296-3644 (Metro) or 1-877-898-9090 (Greater MN)
